heading: Voluntary private sector preparedness accreditation and certification program
label: 321m Voluntary private sector preparedness accreditation and certification program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the officer designated under paragraph (2), shall establish and implement the voluntary private sector preparedness accreditation and certification program in accordance with this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the expertise of the Administrator in emergency management and preparedness in the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the responsibilities of the Administrator as the principal advisor to the President for all matters relating to emergency management in the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: protection of critical infrastructure;

children.label: ii
children.text: risk assessment methodologies; and

children.label: iii
children.text: interacting with the private sector on the issues described in clauses (i) and (ii).

children.label: C
children.text: The Under Secretary for Science and Technology, based on consideration of the expertise of the Under Secretary in, and responsibilities associated with, standards.

children.label: A
children.text: the other officers of the Department referred to in paragraph (2), using the expertise and responsibilities of such officers; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Special Assistant to the Secretary for the Private Sector, based on consideration of the expertise of the Special Assistant in, and responsibilities for, interacting with the private sector.

children.heading: Voluntary private sector preparedness standards; voluntary accreditation and certification program for the private sector
children.label: b Voluntary private sector preparedness standards; voluntary accreditation and certification program for the private sector
A
children.text: begin supporting the development and updating, as necessary, of voluntary preparedness standards through appropriate organizations that coordinate or facilitate the development and use of voluntary consensus standards and voluntary consensus standards development organizations; and

children.label: i
children.text: develop and promote a program to certify the preparedness of private sector entities that voluntarily choose to seek certification under the program; and

children.label: ii
children.text: implement the program under this subsection through any entity with which the designated officer enters into an agreement under paragraph (3)(A), which shall accredit third parties to carry out the certification process under this section.

children.heading: Program
children.label: i Program
children.text: The program developed and implemented under this subsection shall assess whether a private sector entity complies with voluntary preparedness standards.

children.heading: Guidelines
children.label: ii Guidelines
children.text: In developing the program under this subsection, the designated officer shall develop guidelines for the accreditation and certification processes established under this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: shall adopt one or more appropriate voluntary preparedness standards that promote preparedness, which may be tailored to address the unique nature of various sectors within the private sector, as necessary and appropriate, that shall be used in the accreditation and certification program under this subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: after the adoption of one or more standards under clause (i), may adopt additional voluntary preparedness standards or modify or discontinue the use of voluntary preparedness standards for the accreditation and certification program, as necessary and appropriate to promote preparedness.

children.heading: Submission of recommendations
children.label: C Submission of recommendations
children.text: In adopting one or more standards under subparagraph (B), the designated officer may receive recommendations from any entity described in that subparagraph relating to appropriate voluntary preparedness standards, including appropriate sector specific standards, for adoption in the program.

children.heading: Small business concerns
children.label: D Small business concerns
children.text: The designated officer and any entity with which the designated officer enters into an agreement under paragraph (3)(A) shall establish separate classifications and methods of certification for small business concerns (under the meaning given that term in ) for the program under this subsection.section 632 of title 15

children.label: I
children.text: under any other provision of Federal law; or

children.label: II
children.text: by any Sector Risk Management Agency, as defined under Homeland Security Presidential Directive–7; and

children.label: I
children.text: other Department private sector related programs; and

children.label: II
children.text: preparedness and business continuity programs in other Federal agencies.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 210 days after , the designated officer shall enter into one or more agreements with a highly qualified nongovernmental entity with experience or expertise in coordinating and facilitating the development and use of voluntary consensus standards and in managing or implementing accreditation and certification programs for voluntary consensus standards, or a similarly qualified private sector entity, to carry out accreditations and oversee the certification process under this subsection. An entity entering into an agreement with the designated officer under this clause (hereinafter referred to in this section as a “selected entity”) shall not perform certifications under this subsection.August 3, 2007

children.heading: Contents
children.label: ii Contents
children.text: A selected entity shall manage the accreditation process and oversee the certification process in accordance with the program established under this subsection and accredit qualified third parties to carry out the certification program established under this subsection.

children.heading: Procedures and requirements for accreditation and certification
children.label: B Procedures and requirements for accreditation and certification

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any selected entity shall collaborate to develop procedures and requirements for the accreditation and certification processes under this subsection, in accordance with the program established under this subsection and guidelines developed under paragraph (2)(A)(ii).

children.label: I
children.text: ensure reasonable uniformity in any accreditation and certification processes if there is more than one selected entity; and

children.label: II
children.text: be used by any selected entity in conducting accreditations and overseeing the certification process under this subsection.

children.heading: Disagreement
children.label: iii Disagreement
children.text: Any disagreement among selected entities in developing procedures under clause (i) shall be resolved by the designated officer.

children.heading: Designation
children.label: C Designation
children.text: A selected entity may accredit any qualified third party to carry out the certification process under this subsection.

children.heading: Disadvantaged business involvement
children.label: D Disadvantaged business involvement
children.text: In accrediting qualified third parties to carry out the certification process under this subsection, a selected entity shall ensure, to the extent practicable, that the third parties include qualified small, minority, women-owned, or disadvantaged business concerns when appropriate. The term “disadvantaged business concern” means a small business that is owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals, as defined in , United States Code of Federal Regulations.section 124 of title 13

children.heading: Treatment of other certifications
children.label: E Treatment of other certifications
children.text: At the request of any entity seeking certification, any selected entity may consider, as appropriate, other relevant certifications acquired by the entity seeking certification. If the selected entity determines that such other certifications are sufficient to meet the certification requirement or aspects of the certification requirement under this section, the selected entity may give credit to the entity seeking certification, as appropriate, to avoid unnecessarily duplicative certification requirements.

children.label: i
children.text: demonstrate that the third party has the ability to certify private sector entities in accordance with the procedures and requirements developed under subparagraph (B);

children.label: ii
children.text: agree to perform certifications in accordance with such procedures and requirements;

children.label: I
children.text: a private sector entity for which that third party conducts a certification under this subsection; or

children.label: II
children.text: any organization that provides preparedness consulting services to private sector entities;

children.label: iv
children.text: agree not to have any other conflict of interest with respect to any private sector entity for which that third party conducts a certification under this subsection;

children.label: v
children.text: maintain liability insurance coverage at policy limits in accordance with the requirements developed under subparagraph (B); and

children.label: vi
children.text: enter into an agreement with the selected entity accrediting that third party to protect any proprietary information of a private sector entity obtained under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The designated officer and any selected entity shall regularly monitor and inspect the operations of any third party conducting certifications under this subsection to ensure that the third party is complying with the procedures and requirements established under subparagraph (B) and all other applicable requirements.

children.label: I
children.text: revoke the accreditation of that third party to conduct certifications under this subsection; and

children.label: II
children.text: review any certification conducted by that third party, as necessary and appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The designated officer, in consultation with representatives of appropriate organizations that coordinate or facilitate the development and use of voluntary consensus standards, appropriate voluntary consensus standards development organizations, appropriate representatives of State and local governments, including emergency management officials, and each private sector advisory council created under , shall annually review the voluntary accreditation and certification program established under this subsection to ensure the effectiveness of such program (including the operations and management of such program by any selected entity and the selected entity’s inclusion of qualified disadvantaged business concerns under paragraph (3)(D)) and make improvements and adjustments to the program as necessary and appropriate.section 112(f)(4) of this title

children.heading: Review of standards
children.label: B Review of standards
children.text: Each review under subparagraph (A) shall include an assessment of the voluntary preparedness standard or standards used in the program under this subsection.

children.heading: Voluntary participation
children.label: 5 Voluntary participation
children.text: Certification under this subsection shall be voluntary for any private sector entity.

children.heading: Public listing
children.label: 6 Public listing
children.text: The designated officer shall maintain and make public a listing of any private sector entity certified as being in compliance with the program established under this subsection, if that private sector entity consents to such listing.

children.label: A
children.text: under any other provision of federal law; or

children.label: B
children.text: by any Sector Risk Management Agency, as those agencies are defined under Homeland Security Presidential Directive–7; or

exempting any private sector entity seeking certification or meeting certification requirements under subsection (b) from compliance with all applicable statutes, regulations, directives, policies, and industry codes of practice.
